tagline: Agent mode is now the exclusive operational mode.
what_it_does: Genie Code now operates solely in Agent mode, which automates multi-step workflows, retrieves relevant assets, executes code, and improves results based on outputs. Users can still request conversational responses by specifying their preferences in prompts. This change simplifies the user experience by removing the Chat mode selector.
why_it_matters: This update enhances productivity by streamlining the interaction model for developers, allowing them to focus on code generation and optimization without toggling between modes. It benefits teams looking for efficient code assistance and automation in their workflows, ultimately reducing development time and improving code quality.
plain_summary: Genie Code now operates solely in Agent mode, which automates multi step workflows, retrieves relevant assets, executes code, and improves results based on outputs. Users can still request conversational responses by specifying their preferences in prompts.

capabilities.title: Automated Workflows
capabilities.desc: Genie Code automates multi-step workflows and executes code based on user prompts.

capabilities.title: Contextual Responses
capabilities.desc: Users can receive detailed explanations by including specific instructions in their prompts.

capabilities.title: Multi-thread Support
capabilities.desc: Users can manage multiple tasks simultaneously through tabbed threads in the Genie Code pane.

capabilities.title: Approval Management
capabilities.desc: Genie Code requests user approval for tool actions, enhancing control over automated tasks.

prerequisites: Partner-powered AI features enabled for both account and workspace.
Workspace must be in a supported region.
use_cases: Generating and optimizing code in Databricks notebooks.
Debugging code and providing quick fixes.
Retrieving relevant documentation to support coding tasks.
limitations: Genie Code's agentic capabilities require partner-powered AI features; otherwise, it operates with limited functionality.
The auto-approve feature is not a security boundary and should be used cautiously.

steps.title: Open Genie Code Pane
steps.desc: Click the Genie Code icon in the upper-right corner of the page.

steps.title: Enter Prompt
steps.desc: Type your request in the text box and press Enter.

steps.title: Specify Instructions
steps.desc: Include specific instructions in your prompt for conversational responses.

steps.title: Manage Threads
steps.desc: Use tabs to switch between active threads or start new ones.

steps.title: Set Approval Preferences
steps.desc: Adjust approval settings in Genie Code settings for tool actions.

key_takeaway: Genie Code's exclusive shift to Agent mode simplifies user interactions, enhancing automation and productivity for developers.
